mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in nonfarm payrolls indicates rising aggregate wage income, which drives consumption and corporate revenue expectations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS month-over-month increases are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is held below 0.25%, the persistent zero-interest-rate policy suppresses the equity risk premium and discount rate, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as capital is pushed into risk assets.
failure_condition: Periods with FEDFUNDS below 0.25% do not produce statistically significant positive 60-day forward returns compared to periods above 0.25% (t-test p > 0.05) over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in industrial production and declining unemployment reflects operating leverage and labor market tightening that jointly compress corporate cost uncertainty and boost profit expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term of INDPRO momentum and UNRATE change fails to predict positive 20-day returns with an information coefficient above 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During recovery phases, quarterly real GDP growth above its trailing four-quarter average signals demand momentum that outpaces productive capacity, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When GDPC1 exceeds its 4-quarter average during recovery, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 45% of triggered instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in retail sales signals stronger-than-expected consumer spending that immediately lifts near-term revenue forecasts, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month increases in RSAFS are followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production signals weakening manufacturing activity and inventory accumulation that forces near-term earnings downgrades, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month declines in INDPRO are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An accelerating consumer price index erodes real corporate earnings and heightens the risk of Federal Reserve tightening, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL acceleration above 3.0% annualized is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of triggered periods over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in housing starts signals residential investment momentum that generates backward linkages to construction, durable goods, and credit expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST above 900k units fails to produce positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of triggered quarters over a 3-year rolling window.
